key_insight: PREDIKTOR predicts patient drug response by aligning a personalized tumor knowledge graph with a simulated transcriptomic perturbation view in a shared latent space.
clinical_relevance: This could help identify which cancer treatments are more likely to work for a specific patient before therapy, with added interpretability for mechanism-based decision support.
method: Builds a patient-specific gene regulatory network from tumor expression and adds drug-target links from DrugBank.
Uses a graph neural encoder to create a mechanistically grounded drug-patient embedding.
Aligns that embedding with a frozen LINCS-based gene-level perturbation model using contrastive learning, then classifies response.
evidence_signal: Across TCGA patient-, drug-, and tissue-split tests, the model outperformed baselines and also transferred zero-shot to I-SPY2 with a 5.6% AUROC gain.
watch_item: The framework depends on high-quality transcriptomic data and pretrained perturbation models, so performance may vary when patient data are sparse, noisy, or out of distribution.
paper_title: Predicting Therapeutic Outcome via Aligning Patient-Specific Knowledge Graph and Gene-Level Perturbation Representations
